title: Ancient Greece–Ancient India relations
body: For the ancient Greeks, India (Greek: Ἰνδία) referred to the geographical region located east of Persia and south of the Himalayas, excluding Serica. At later points in history, the term also came to refer either to the more extensive Indian subcontinent or to the less extensive Indus Plain.
categories: All Wikipedia articles written in British English with Oxford spelling
